<a id="configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers--implicit-request-handlers"></a>

# Implicit Request Handlers

Solr ships with many out-of-the-box request handlers, which are called implicit because they do not need to be configured in `solrconfig.xml` before you are able to use them.

These handlers have pre-defined default parameters, known as *paramsets*, which can be modified if necessary.

<a id="configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers--available-implicit-endpoints"></a>

## Available Implicit Endpoints

> [!NOTE]
> All endpoint paths listed below should be placed after Solr’s host and port (if a port is used) to construct a URL.

<a id="configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers--admin-handlers"></a>

### Admin Handlers

Many of these handlers are used throughout the Admin UI to show information about Solr.

File

Return content of files in `${solr.home}/conf/`.
This handler must have a collection name in the path to the endpoint.

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/admin/file` | [ShowFileRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/admin/ShowFileRequestHandler.html) | `_ADMIN_FILE`

Health

Report the health of the node (*available only in SolrCloud mode*)

| API Endpoints | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| v1: `solr/admin/info/health` v2: `api/node/health` | [HealthCheckHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/admin/HealthCheckHandler.html) |  |

This endpoint also accepts additional request parameters.
Please see [Javadocs](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/admin/HealthCheckHandler.html) for details.

Logging

Retrieve and modify registered loggers.

| API Endpoints | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| v1: `solr/admin/info/logging` v2: `api/node/logging` | [LoggingHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/admin/LoggingHandler.html) | `_ADMIN_LOGGING`

Luke

Expose the internal Lucene index.
This handler must have a collection name in the path to the endpoint.

**Documentation**: [Luke Request Handler](#indexing-guide-luke-request-handler)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/admin/luke` | [LukeRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/admin/LukeRequestHandler.html) | `_ADMIN_LUKE`

Ping

Health check.
This handler must have a collection name in the path to the endpoint.

**Documentation**: [Ping](#deployment-guide-ping)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/admin/ping` | [PingRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/PingRequestHandler.html) | `_ADMIN_PING`

System Properties

Return JRE system properties.

| API Endpoints | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| v1: `solr/admin/info/properties` v2: `api/node/properties` | [PropertiesRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/admin/PropertiesRequestHandler.html) | `_ADMIN_PROPERTIES`

Segments

Return info on last commit generation Lucene index segments.

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/admin/segments` | [SegmentsInfoRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/admin/SegmentsInfoRequestHandler.html) | `_ADMIN_SEGMENTS`

System Info

Return server statistics and settings.

**Documentation**: [System Info Handler](#configuration-guide-system-info-handler)

| API Endpoints | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| v1: `solr/admin/info/system` v2: `api/node/system` | [SystemInfoHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/admin/SystemInfoHandler.html) | `_ADMIN_SYSTEM` |

This endpoint can also take the collection or core name in the path (`solr/<collection>/admin/system` or `solr/<core>/admin/system`) which will include all of the system-level information and additional information about the specific core that served the request.

Threads

Return info on all JVM threads.

| API Endpoints | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| v1: `solr/admin/info/threads` v2: `api/node/threads` | [ThreadDumpHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/admin/ThreadDumpHandler.html) | `_ADMIN_THREADS`

<a id="configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers--analysis-handlers"></a>

### Analysis Handlers

Document Analysis

Return a breakdown of the analysis process of the given document.

**Documentation**: <https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/solr/AnalysisRequestHandler>

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/analysis/document` | [DocumentAnalysisRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/DocumentAnalysisRequestHandler.html) | `_ANALYSIS_DOCUMENT`

Field Analysis

Return index- and query-time analysis over the given field(s)/field type(s).
This handler drives the [Analysis Screen](#indexing-guide-analysis-screen) in Solr’s Admin UI.

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/analysis/field` | [FieldAnalysisRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/FieldAnalysisRequestHandler.html) | `_ANALYSIS_FIELD`

<a id="configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers--handlers-for-configuration"></a>

### Handlers for Configuration

Config API

Retrieve and modify Solr configuration.

**Documentation**: [Config API](#configuration-guide-config-api)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| v1: `solr/<collection>/config` v2: `api/collections/<collection>/config` | [SolrConfigHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/SolrConfigHandler.html) | `_CONFIG`

Dump

Echo the request contents back to the client.

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/debug/dump` | [DumpRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/DumpRequestHandler.html) | `_DEBUG_DUMP`

Replication

Replicate indexes for SolrCloud recovery and user-managed cluster index distribution.
This handler must have a core name in the path to the endpoint.

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<core>/replication` | [ReplicationHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/ReplicationHandler.html) | `_REPLICATION`

Schema API

Retrieve and modify the Solr schema.

**Documentation**: [Schema API](#indexing-guide-schema-api)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| v1: `solr/<collection>/schema`, `solr/<core>/schema` v2: `api/collections/<collection>/schema`, `api/cores/<core>/schema` | [SchemaHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/SchemaHandler.html) | `_SCHEMA`

<a id="configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers--query-handlers"></a>

### Query Handlers

Export

Export full sorted result sets.

**Documentation**: [Exporting Result Sets](#query-guide-exporting-result-sets)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/export` | [ExportHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/ExportHandler.html) | `_EXPORT`

RealTimeGet

Low-latency retrieval of the latest version of a document.

**Documentation**: [RealTime Get](#configuration-guide-realtime-get)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/get` | [RealTimeGetHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/RealTimeGetHandler.html) | `_GET`

Graph Traversal

Return [GraphML](http://graphml.graphdrawing.org/) formatted output from a `gatherNodes` streaming expression.

**Documentation**: [Graph Traversal](#query-guide-graph-traversal)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/graph` | [GraphHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/GraphHandler.html) | `_ADMIN_GRAPH`

SQL

SQL query support.

**Documentation**: [SQL Request Handler](#query-guide-sql-query--sql-request-handler)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/sql` | [SQLHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/modules/sql/org/apache/solr/handler/sql/SQLHandler.html) | `_SQL`

Streaming Expressions

Distributed stream processing.

**Documentation**: [Streaming Requests and Responses](#query-guide-streaming-expressions--streaming-requests-and-responses)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/stream` | [StreamHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/StreamHandler.html) | `_STREAM`

Terms

Return a field’s indexed terms and the number of documents containing each term.

**Documentation**: [Using the Terms Component in a Request Handler](#query-guide-terms-component--using-the-terms-component-in-a-request-handler)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/terms` | [SearchHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/component/SearchHandler.html) | `_TERMS`

<a id="configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers--update-handlers"></a>

### Update Handlers

Update

Add, delete and update indexed documents formatted as SolrXML, CSV, SolrJSON or javabin.

**Documentation**: [Indexing with Update Handlers](#indexing-guide-indexing-with-update-handlers)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/update` | [UpdateRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/UpdateRequestHandler.html) | `_UPDATE`

CSV Updates

Add and update CSV-formatted documents.

**Documentation**: [CSV Update Convenience Paths](#indexing-guide-indexing-with-update-handlers--csv-update-convenience-paths)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/update/csv` | [UpdateRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/UpdateRequestHandler.html) | `_UPDATE_CSV`

JSON Updates

Add, delete and update SolrJSON-formatted documents.

**Documentation**: [JSON Update Convenience Paths](#indexing-guide-indexing-with-update-handlers--json-update-convenience-paths)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/update/json` | [UpdateRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/UpdateRequestHandler.html) | `_UPDATE_JSON`

Custom JSON Updates

Add and update custom JSON-formatted documents.

**Documentation**: [Transforming and Indexing Custom JSON](#indexing-guide-transforming-and-indexing-custom-json)

| API Endpoint | Class & Javadocs | Paramset |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `solr/<collection>/update/json/docs` | [UpdateRequestHandler](https://solr.apache.org/docs/10_0_0/core/org/apache/solr/handler/UpdateRequestHandler.html) | `_UPDATE_JSON_DOCS`

<a id="configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers--how-to-view-implicit-handler-paramsets"></a>

## How to View Implicit Handler Paramsets

You can see configuration for all request handlers, including the implicit request handlers, via the [Config API](#configuration-guide-config-api).

To include the expanded paramset in the response, as well as the effective parameters from merging the paramset parameters with the built-in parameters, use the `expandParams` request parameter.

For example, for the `/export` request handler, you can make a request like this:

- V1 API
- V2 API

```bash
http://localhost:8983/solr/gettingstarted/config/requestHandler?componentName=/export&expandParams=true
```

```bash
http://localhost:8983/api/collections/gettingstarted/config/requestHandler?componentName=/export&expandParams=true
```

The response will look similar to:

```json
{"config": {"requestHandler": {"/export": {"class": "solr.ExportHandler","useParams": "_EXPORT","components": ["query"],"defaults": {"wt": "json" },"invariants": {"rq": "{!xport}","distrib": false },"name": "/export","_useParamsExpanded_": {"_EXPORT": "[NOT AVAILABLE]" },"_effectiveParams_": {"distrib": "false","omitHeader": "true","wt": "json","rq": "{!xport}"}}}}}
```

<a id="configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers--how-to-edit-implicit-handler-paramsets"></a>

## How to Edit Implicit Handler Paramsets

Because implicit request handlers are not present in `solrconfig.xml`, configuration of their associated `default`, `invariant` and `appends` parameters may be edited via the [Request Parameters API](#configuration-guide-request-parameters-api) using the paramset listed in the above table.
However, other parameters, including SearchHandler components, may not be modified.
The invariants and appends specified in the implicit configuration cannot be overridden.

---

<a id="configuration-guide-realtime-get"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/latest/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html -->

<!-- page_index: 89 -->

# RealTime Get

10.0

[10.0](#configuration-guide-realtime-get)
[9.10](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_10/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.9](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_9/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.8](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_8/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.7](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_7/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.6](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_6/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.5](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_5/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.4](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_4/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.3](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_3/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.2](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_2/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.1](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_1/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)
[9.0](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_0/configuration-guide/realtime-get.html)

[Edit this Page](https://github.com/apache/solr/tree/main/solr/solr-ref-guide/modules/configuration-guide/pages/realtime-get.adoc)

<a id="configuration-guide-realtime-get--realtime-get"></a>

# RealTime Get

For index updates to be visible (searchable), some kind of commit must reopen a searcher to a new point-in-time view of the index.

The **realtime get** feature allows retrieval (by `unique-key`) of the latest version of any documents without the associated cost of reopening a searcher.
This is primarily useful when using Solr as a NoSQL data store and not just a search index.

Real Time Get relies on the update log feature, which is enabled by default and can be configured in `solrconfig.xml`:

```xml
<updateLog>
  <str name="dir">${solr.ulog.dir:}</str>
</updateLog>
```

Real Time Get requests can be performed using the `/get` handler which exists implicitly in Solr - see [Implicit Request Handlers](#configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers) - it’s equivalent to the following configuration:

```xml
<requestHandler name="/get" class="solr.RealTimeGetHandler">
  <lst name="defaults">
    <str name="omitHeader">true</str>
  </lst>
</requestHandler>
```

For example, if you started Solr using the `bin/solr start -e techproducts` example command, you could then index a new document without committing it, like so:

```bash
curl 'http://localhost:8983/solr/techproducts/update/json?commitWithin=10000000' \
  -H 'Content-type:application/json' -d '[{"id":"mydoc","name":"realtime-get test!"}]'
```

If you search for this document, it should not be found yet:

```bash
http://localhost:8983/solr/techproducts/query?q=id:mydoc
```

```json
{"response":
  {"numFound":0,"start":0,"docs":[]}
}
```

However if you use the Real Time Get handler exposed at `/get`, you can retrieve the document:

- V1 API
- V2 API

```bash
http://localhost:8983/solr/techproducts/get?id=mydoc
```

```json
{"doc": {
  "id": "mydoc",
  "name": "realtime-get test!",
  "_version_": 1487137811571146752
  }
}
```

```bash
http://localhost:8983/api/collections/techproducts/get?id=mydoc
```

```json
{"doc": {
  "id": "mydoc",
  "name": "realtime-get test!",
  "_version_": 1487137811571146752
  }
}
```

You can also specify multiple documents at once via the `ids` parameter and a comma separated list of ids, or by using multiple `id` parameters.
If you specify multiple ids, or use the `ids` parameter, the response will mimic a normal query response to make it easier for existing clients to parse.

For example:

- V1 API
- V2 API

```bash
http://localhost:8983/solr/techproducts/get?ids=mydoc,IW-02
http://localhost:8983/solr/techproducts/get?id=mydoc&id=IW-02
```

```json
{"response":{"numFound":2,"start":0,"docs":[ { "id":"mydoc","name":"realtime-get test!","_version_":1487137811571146752},{"id":"IW-02","name":"iPod & iPod Mini USB 2.0 Cable"}]}}
```

```bash
http://localhost:8983/api/collections/techproducts/get?ids=mydoc,IW-02
http://localhost:8983/api/collections/techproducts/get?id=mydoc&id=IW-02
```

```json
{"response":{"numFound":2,"start":0,"docs":[ { "id":"mydoc","name":"realtime-get test!","_version_":1487137811571146752},{"id":"IW-02","name":"iPod & iPod Mini USB 2.0 Cable"}]}}
```

Real Time Get requests can also be combined with filter queries, specified with an [`fq` parameter](#query-guide-common-query-parameters--fq-filter-query-parameter):

- V1 API
- V2 API

```bash
http://localhost:8983/solr/techproducts/get?id=mydoc&id=IW-02&fq=name:realtime-get
```

```json
{"response":{"numFound":1,"start":0,"docs":[ { "id":"mydoc","name":"realtime-get test!","_version_":1487137811571146752}]}}
```

```bash
http://localhost:8983/api/collections/techproducts/get?id=mydoc&id=IW-02&fq=name:realtime-get
```

```json
{"response":{"numFound":1,"start":0,"docs":[ { "id":"mydoc","name":"realtime-get test!","_version_":1487137811571146752}]}}
```

> [!WARNING]
> Do **NOT** disable the realtime get handler at `/get` if you are using SolrCloud.
> Doing so will result in any leader election to cause a full sync in **ALL** replicas for the shard in question.
>
> Similarly, a replica recovery will also always fetch the complete index from the leader because a partial sync will not be possible in the absence of this handler.

---

# InitParams

10.0

[10.0](#configuration-guide-initparams)
[9.10](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_10/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.9](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_9/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.8](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_8/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.7](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_7/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.6](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_6/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.5](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_5/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.4](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_4/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.3](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_3/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.2](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_2/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.1](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_1/configuration-guide/initparams.html)
[9.0](https://solr.apache.org/guide/solr/9_0/configuration-guide/initparams.html)

[Edit this Page](https://github.com/apache/solr/tree/main/solr/solr-ref-guide/modules/configuration-guide/pages/initparams.adoc)

<a id="configuration-guide-initparams--initparams"></a>

# InitParams

The `<initParams>` section of `solrconfig.xml` allows you to define request handler parameters outside of the handler configuration.

There are a couple of use cases where this might be desired:

- Some handlers are implicitly defined in code (see [Implicit Request Handlers](#configuration-guide-implicit-requesthandlers)) and there should be a way to add, append, or override some of the implicitly defined properties.
- There are a few properties that are used across handlers.
  This helps you keep only a single definition of those properties and apply them over multiple handlers.

For example, if you want several of your search handlers to return the same list of fields, you can create an `<initParams>` section without having to define the same set of parameters in each request handler definition.
If you have a single request handler that should return different fields, you can define the overriding parameters in individual `<requestHandler>` sections as usual.

The properties and configuration of an `<initParams>` section mirror the properties and configuration of a request handler.
It can include sections for defaults, appends, and invariants, the same as any request handler.

For example, here is one of the `<initParams>` sections defined by default in the `_default` example:

```xml
<initParams path="/update/**,/query,/select,/tvrh,/elevate,/spell">
  <lst name="defaults">
    <str name="df">_text_</str>
  </lst>
</initParams>
```

This sets the default search field ("df") to be "*text*" for all of the request handlers named in the path section.
If we later want to change the `/query` request handler to search a different field by default, we could override the `<initParams>` by defining the parameter in the `<requestHandler>` section for `/query`.

The syntax and semantics are similar to that of a `<requestHandler>`.
The following are the attributes:

`path`
:

Optional

Default: none

    A comma-separated list of paths which will use the parameters.
    Wildcards can be used in paths to define nested paths, as described below.

`name`
:

Optional

Default: none

    The name of this set of parameters.
    The name can be used directly in a requestHandler definition if a path is not explicitly named.
    If you give your `<initParams>` a name, you can refer to the parameters in a `<requestHandler>` that is not defined as a path.

    For example, if an `<initParams>` section has the name "myParams", you can call the name when defining your request handler:


```xml
<requestHandler name="/dump1" class="DumpRequestHandler" initParams="myParams"/>
```

<a id="configuration-guide-initparams--wildcards-in-initparams"></a>

## Wildcards in initParams

An `<initParams>` section can support wildcards to define nested paths that should use the parameters defined.
A single asterisk (`*`) denotes that a nested path one level deeper should use the parameters.
Double asterisks (`**`) denote all nested paths no matter how deep should use the parameters.

For example, if we have an `<initParams>` that looks like this:

```xml
<initParams name="myParams" path="/myhandler,/root/*,/root1/**">
  <lst name="defaults">
    <str name="fl">_text_</str>
  </lst>
  <lst name="invariants">
    <str name="rows">10</str>
  </lst>
  <lst name="appends">
    <str name="df">title</str>
  </lst>
</initParams>
```

We’ve defined three paths in the first line of the example:

- `/myhandler` declared as a direct path.
- `/root/*` with a single asterisk to indicate the parameters should apply to paths that are one level deep.
- `/root1/**` with double asterisks to indicate the parameters should apply to all nested paths, no matter how deep.

When we define the request handlers, the wildcards will work in the following ways:

```xml
<requestHandler name="/myhandler" class="SearchHandler"/>
```

The `/myhandler` class was named as a path in the `<initParams>` so this will use those parameters.

Next we have a request handler named `/root/search5`:

```xml
<requestHandler name="/root/search5" class="SearchHandler"/>
```

We defined a wildcard for nested paths that are one level deeper than `/root`, so this request handler will use the parameters.
This one, however, will not, because `/root/search5/test` is more than one level deep from `/root`:

```xml
<requestHandler name="/root/search5/test" class="SearchHandler"/>
```

If we want to define all levels of nested paths, we should use double asterisks, as in the example path `/root1/**`:

```xml
<requestHandler name="/root1/search/tests" class="SearchHandler"/>
```

Any path under `/root1`, whether explicitly defined in a request handler or not, will use the parameters defined in the matching `initParams` section.

---
