<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--constantdistribution"></a>

## constantDistribution

The `constantDistribution` function returns a constant probability distribution based on its parameter.
This function is part of the probability distribution framework and is designed to work with the `sample` and `cumulativeProbability` functions.

When sampled the constant distribution always returns its constant value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--constantdistribution-parameters"></a>

### constantDistribution Parameters

- `double`: constant value

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--constantdistribution-returns"></a>

### constantDistribution Returns

A probability distribution function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--constantdistribution-syntax"></a>

### constantDistribution Syntax

```text
constantDistribution(constantValue)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--conv"></a>

## conv

The `conv` function returns the [convolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Convolution) of two numeric arrays.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--conv-parameters"></a>

### conv Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--conv-syntax"></a>

### conv Syntax

```text
conv(numericArray1, numericArray2)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--copyof"></a>

## copyOf

The `copyOf` function creates a copy of a numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--copyof-parameters"></a>

### copyOf Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `length`: The length of the copied array.
  The returned array will be right padded with zeros if the length parameter exceeds the size of the original array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--copyof-syntax"></a>

### copyOf Syntax

```text
copyOf(numericArray, length)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--copyofrange"></a>

## copyOfRange

The `copyOfRange` function creates a copy of a range of a numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--copyofrange-parameters"></a>

### copyOfRange Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `start index`
- `end index`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--copyofrange-syntax"></a>

### copyOfRange Syntax

```text
copyOfRange(numericArray, startIndex, endIndex)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--corr"></a>

## corr

The `corr` function returns the correlation of two numeric arrays or the correlation matrix for a matrix.

The `corr` function support Pearson’s, Kendall’s and Spearman’s correlations.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--corr-positional-parameters"></a>

### corr Positional Parameters

- `numeric array`: The first numeric array
- `numeric array`: The second numeric array

OR

- `matrix`: The matrix to compute the correlation matrix for.
  Note that correlation is computed between the `columns` in the matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--corr-named-parameters"></a>

### corr Named Parameters

- `type`: (Optional) The type of correlation.
  Possible values are `pearsons`, `kendalls`, or `spearmans`.
  The default is `pearsons`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--corr-syntax"></a>

### corr Syntax

```text
corr(numericArray1, numericArray2) // Compute the Pearsons correlation for two numeric arrays
corr(numericArray1, numericArray2, type=kendalls) // Compute the Kendalls correlation for two numeric arrays
corr(matrix) // Compute the Pearsons correlation matrix for a matrix
corr(matrix, type=spearmans) // Compute the Spearmans correlation matrix for a matrix
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--corr-returns"></a>

### corr Returns

number | matrix: Either the correlation or correlation matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cos"></a>

## cos

The `cos` function returns the trigonometric cosine of a number.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cos-parameters"></a>

### cos Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`: The value to return the hyperbolic cosine of.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cos-syntax"></a>

### cos Syntax

```text
cos(100.4)  // returns the arccosine of 100.4
cos(fieldA) // returns the arccosine for fieldA.
if(gt(fieldA,fieldB),cos(fieldA),cos(fieldB)) // if fieldA > fieldB then return the arccosine of fieldA, else return the cosine of fieldB
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cosinesimilarity"></a>

## cosineSimilarity

The `cosineSimilarity` function returns the [cosine similarity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cosine_similarity) of two numeric arrays.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cosinesimilarity-parameters"></a>

### cosineSimilarity Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cosinesimilarity-returns"></a>

### cosineSimilarity Returns

A numeric.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cosinesimilarity-syntax"></a>

### cosineSimilarity Syntax

```text
cosineSimilarity(numericArray, numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cov"></a>

## cov

The `cov` function returns the covariance of two numeric array or the covariance matrix for matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cov-parameters"></a>

### cov Parameters

- `numeric array`: The first numeric array
- `numeric array`: The second numeric array

OR

- `matrix`: The matrix to compute the covariance matrix from.
  Note that covariance is computed between the `columns` in the matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cov-syntax"></a>

### cov Syntax

```text
cov(numericArray, numericArray) // Computes the covariance of a two numeric arrays
cov(matrix) // Computes the covariance matrix for the matrix.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cov-returns"></a>

### cov Returns

number | matrix: Either the covariance or covariance matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cumulativeprobability"></a>

## cumulativeProbability

The `cumulativeProbability` function returns the cumulative probability of a random variable within a probability distribution.
The cumulative probability is the total probability of all random variables less than or equal to a random variable.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cumulativeprobability-parameters"></a>

### cumulativeProbability Parameters

- `probability distribution`
- `number`: Value to compute the probability for.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cumulativeprobability-returns"></a>

### cumulativeProbability Returns

A double: the cumulative probability.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cumulativeprobability-syntax"></a>

### cumulativeProbability Syntax

```text
cumulativeProbability(normalDistribution(500, 25), 502) // Returns the cumulative probability of the random sample 502 in a normal distribution with a mean of 500 and standard deviation of 25.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--derivative"></a>

## derivative

The `derivative` function returns the [derivative](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Derivative) of a function.
The derivative function can compute the derivative of the [spline](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--spline) function and the [loess](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--loess) function.
The derivative can also take the derivative of a derivative.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--derivative-parameters"></a>

### derivative Parameters

- `spline` | `loess` | `akima` | `lerp` | `derivative`: The functions to compute the derivative for.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--derivative-syntax"></a>

### derivative Syntax

```text
derivative(spline(...))
derivative(loess(...))
derivative(derivative(...))
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--derivative-returns"></a>

### derivative Returns

function: The function can be treated as both a `numeric array` and `function`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--describe"></a>

## describe

The `describe` function returns a tuple containing the descriptive statistics for an array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--describe-parameters"></a>

### describe Parameters

- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--describe-syntax"></a>

### describe Syntax

```text
describe(numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--diff"></a>

## diff

The `diff` functions performs [time series differencing](https://www.otexts.org/fpp/8/1).

Time series differencing is often used to make a time series stationary before further analysis.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--diff-parameters"></a>

### diff Parameters

- `numeric array`: The time series data.
- `integer`: (Optional) The lag.
  Defaults to 1.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--diff-syntax"></a>

### diff Syntax

```text
diff(numericArray1) // Perform time series differencing with a default lag of 1.
diff(numericArray1, 30) // Perform time series differencing with a lag of 30.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--diff-returns"></a>

### diff Returns

numeric array: The differenced time series data.
The size of the array will be equal to (original array size - lag).

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--distance"></a>

## distance

The `distance` function computes the distance of two numeric arrays or the distance matrix for a matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--distance-positional-parameters"></a>

### distance Positional Parameters

- `numeric array`: The first numeric array
- `numeric array`: The second numeric array

OR

- `matrix`: The matrix to compute the distance matrix for.
  Note that distance is computed between the `columns` in the matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--distance-named-parameters"></a>

### distance Named Parameters

- `type`: (Optional) The distance type.
  Possible values are `euclidean`, `manhattan`, `canberra`, or `earthMovers`.
  The default is `euclidean`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--distance-syntax"></a>

### distance Syntax

```text
distance(numericArray1, numericArray2) // Computes the euclidean distance for two numeric arrays.
distance(numericArray1, numericArray2, type=manhattan) // Computes the manhattan distance for two numeric arrays.
distance(matrix) // Computes the euclidean distance matrix for a matrix.
distance(matrix, type=canberra) // Computes the canberra distance matrix for a matrix.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--distance-returns"></a>

### distance Returns

number | matrix: Either the distance or distance matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--div"></a>

## div

The `div` function will take two numeric values and divide them.
The function will fail to execute if any of the values are non-numeric or null, or the 2nd value is 0.
Returns a numeric value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--div-parameters"></a>

### div Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--div-syntax"></a>

### div Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `div` evaluator.
The first value will be divided by the second and as such the second cannot be 0.

```text
div(1,2) // 1 / 2
div(1,fieldA) // 1 / fieldA
div(fieldA,1.4) // fieldA / 1.4
div(fieldA,add(fieldA,fieldB)) // fieldA / (fieldA + fieldB)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--dotproduct"></a>

## dotProduct

The `dotProduct` function returns the [dotproduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dot_product) of two numeric arrays.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--dotproduct-parameters"></a>

### dotProduct Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--dotproduct-returns"></a>

### dotProduct Returns

A number.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--dotproduct-syntax"></a>

### dotProduct Syntax

```text
dotProduct(numericArray, numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebeadd"></a>

## ebeAdd

The `ebeAdd` function performs an element-by-element addition of two numeric arrays.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebeadd-parameters"></a>

### ebeAdd Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebeadd-returns"></a>

### ebeAdd Returns

A numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebeadd-syntax"></a>

### ebeAdd Syntax

```text
ebeAdd(numericArray, numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebedivide"></a>

## ebeDivide

The `ebeDivide` function performs an element-by-element division of two numeric arrays.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebedivide-parameters"></a>

### ebeDivide Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebedivide-returns"></a>

### ebeDivide Returns

A numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebedivide-syntax"></a>

### ebeDivide Syntax

```text
ebeDivide(numericArray, numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebemultiple"></a>

## ebeMultiple

The `ebeMultiply` function performs an element-by-element multiplication of two numeric arrays.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebemultiply-parameters"></a>

### ebeMultiply Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebemultiply-returns"></a>

### ebeMultiply Returns

A numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebemultiply-syntax"></a>

### ebeMultiply Syntax

```text
ebeMultiply(numericArray, numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebesubtract"></a>

## ebeSubtract

The `ebeSubtract` function performs an element-by-element subtraction of two numeric arrays.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebesubtract-parameters"></a>

### ebeSubtract Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebesubtract-returns"></a>

### ebeSubtract Returns

A numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--ebesubtract-syntax"></a>

### ebeSubtract Syntax

```text
ebeSubtract(numericArray, numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--empiricaldistribution"></a>

## empiricalDistribution

The `empiricalDistribution` function returns [empirical distribution function](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Empirical_distribution_function), a continuous probability distribution function based on an actual data set.
This function is part of the probability distribution framework and is designed to work with the `sample`, `kolmogorovSmirnov` and `cumulativeProbability` functions.

This function is designed to work with continuous data.
To build a distribution from
a discrete data set use the `enumeratedDistribution`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--empiricaldistribution-parameters"></a>

### empiricalDistribution Parameters

- `numeric array`: empirical observations

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--empiricaldistribution-returns"></a>

### empiricalDistribution Returns

A probability distribution function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--empiricaldistribution-syntax"></a>

### empiricalDistribution Syntax

```text
empiricalDistribution(numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--enumerateddistribution"></a>

## enumeratedDistribution

The `enumeratedDistribution` function returns a discrete probability distribution function based on an actual data set or a pre-defined set of data and probabilities.
This function is part of the probability distribution framework and is designed to work with the `sample`, `probability` and `cumulativeProbability` functions.

The enumeratedDistribution can be called in two different scenarios:

1. Single array of discrete values.
   This works like an empirical distribution for discrete data.
2. An array of singleton discrete values and an array of double values representing the probabilities of the discrete values.

This function is designed to work with discrete data.
To build a distribution from a continuous data set use the `empiricalDistribution`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--enumerateddistribution-parameters"></a>

### enumeratedDistribution Parameters

- `integer array`: discrete observations or singleton discrete values.
- `double array`: (Optional) values representing the probabilities of the singleton discrete values.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--enumerateddistribution-returns"></a>

### enumeratedDistribution Returns

A probability distribution function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--enumerateddistribution-syntax"></a>

### enumeratedDistribution Syntax

```text
enumeratedDistribution(integerArray) // This creates an enumerated distribution from the observations in the numeric array.
enumeratedDistribution(array(1,2,3,4), array(.25,.25,.25,.25)) // This creates an enumerated distribution with four discrete values (1,2,3,4) each with a probability of .25.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--eor"></a>

## eor

The `eor` function will return the logical exclusive or of at least two boolean parameters.
The function will fail to execute if any parameters are non-boolean or null.
Returns a boolean value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--eor-parameters"></a>

### eor Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Boolean | Boolean Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Boolean | Boolean Evaluator`
- `……`
- `Field Name | Raw Boolean | Boolean Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--eor-syntax"></a>

### eor Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `eor` evaluator.
At least two parameters are required, but there is no limit to how many you can use.

```text
eor(true,fieldA) // true iff fieldA is false
eor(fieldA,fieldB) // true iff either fieldA or fieldB is true but not both
eor(eq(fieldA,fieldB),eq(fieldC,fieldD)) // true iff either fieldA == fieldB or fieldC == fieldD but not both
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--eq"></a>

## eq

The `eq` function will return whether all the parameters are equal, as per Java’s standard `equals(…)` function.
The function accepts parameters of any type, but will fail to execute if all the parameters are not of the same type.
That is, all are Boolean, all are String, or all are Numeric.
If any parameters are null and there is at least one parameter that is not null then false will be returned.
Returns a boolean value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--eq-parameters"></a>

### eq Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`
- `……`
- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--eq-syntax"></a>

### eq Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `eq` evaluator.

```text
eq(1,2) // 1 == 2
eq(1,fieldA) // 1 == fieldA
eq(fieldA,val(foo)) fieldA == "foo"
eq(add(fieldA,fieldB),6) // fieldA + fieldB == 6
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--expmovingage"></a>

## expMovingAge

The `expMovingAverage` function computes an [exponential moving average](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moving_average#Exponential_moving_average) for a numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--expmovingage-parameters"></a>

### expMovingAge Parameters

- `numeric array`: The array to compute the exponential moving average from.
- `integer`: window size

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--expmovingavg-returns"></a>

### expMovingAvg Returns

A numeric array.
The first element of the returned array will start from the windowSize-1 index of the original array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--expmovingavg-syntax"></a>

### expMovingAvg Syntax

```text
expMovingAvg(numericArray, 5) //Computes an exponential moving average with a window size of 5.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--factorial"></a>

## factorial

The `factorial` function returns the [factorial](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Factorial) of its parameter.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--factorial-parameters"></a>

### factorial Parameters

- `integer`: The value to compute the factorial for.
  The largest supported value of this parameter is 170.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--factorial-returns"></a>

### factorial Returns

A double.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--factorial-syntax"></a>

### factorial Syntax

```text
factorial(100) //Computes the factorial of 100
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--finddelay"></a>

## finddelay

The `finddelay` function performs a cross-correlation between two numeric arrays and returns the delay.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--finddelay-parameters"></a>

### finddelay Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--finddelay-syntax"></a>

### finddelay Syntax

```text
finddelay(numericArray1, numericArray2)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--floor"></a>

## floor

The `floor` function rounds a decimal value to the next lowest whole number.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--floor-parameters"></a>

### floor Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`: The decimal to round down.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--floor-syntax"></a>

### floor Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `floor` evaluator.

```text
floor(100.4) // returns 100.
ceil(fieldA) // returns the next lowestt whole number for fieldA.
if(gt(fieldA,fieldB),floor(fieldA),floor(fieldB)) // if fieldA > fieldB then return the floor of fieldA, else return the floor of fieldB.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--freqtable"></a>

## freqTable

The `freqTable` function returns a [frequency distribution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frequency_distribution) from an array of discrete values.

This function is designed to work with discrete values.
To work with continuous data use the `hist` function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--freqtable-parameters"></a>

### freqTable Parameters

- `integer array`: The values to build the frequency distribution from.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--freqtable-returns"></a>

### freqTable Returns

A list of tuples containing the frequency information for each discrete value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--freqtable-syntax"></a>

### freqTable Syntax

```text
freqTable(integerArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gammadistribution"></a>

## gammaDistribution

The `gammaDistribution` function returns a [gamma probability distribution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gamma_distribution) based on its parameters.
This function is part of the probability distribution framework and is designed to work with the `sample`, `kolmogorovSmirnov` and `cumulativeProbability` functions.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gammadistribution-parameters"></a>

### gammaDistribution Parameters

- `double`: shape
- `double`: scale

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gammadistribution-returns"></a>

### gammaDistribution Returns

A probability distribution function,

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gammadistribution-syntax"></a>

### gammaDistribution Syntax

```text
gammaDistribution(1, 10)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--geometricdistribution"></a>

## geometricDistribution

The `geometricDistribution` function returns a [geometric probability distribution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geometric_distribution) based on its parameters.
This function is part of the probability distribution framework and is designed to work with the [sample](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sample), [probability](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--probability) and [cumulativeProbability](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--cumulativeprobability) functions.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--geometricdistribution-parameters"></a>

### geometricDistribution Parameters

- `double`: probability

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--geometricdistribution-syntax"></a>

### geometricDistribution Syntax

```text
geometricDistribution(.5) // Creates a geometric distribution with probability of .5
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--geometricdistribution-returns"></a>

### geometricDistribution Returns

A probability distribution function

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getattribute"></a>

## getAttribute

The `getAttribute` function returns an attribute from a `matrix` by its key.
Any function that returns a `matrix` can also set attributes on the `matrix` with additional information.
The `setAttribute` function can also be used to set attributes on a `matrix`.
The key to an attribute is always a string.
The value of attribute can be any object including numerics, arrays, maps, matrixes, etc.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getattribute-parameters"></a>

### getAttribute Parameters

- `matrix`: The matrix to set the attribute on
- `string`: The key for the attribute

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getattribute-syntax"></a>

### getAttribute Syntax

```text
getAttribute(matrix, key)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getattribute-returns"></a>

### getAttribute Returns

object: any object

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getattributes"></a>

## getAttributes

The `getAttributes` function returns the attribute map from matrix.
See the `getAttribute` function for more details on attributes.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getattributes-parameters"></a>

### getAttributes Parameters

- `matrix`: The matrix to retrieve the attribute map from.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getattributes-syntax"></a>
<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getattributes-syntax:"></a>

### getAttributes Syntax:

```text
getAttributes(matrix)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getattributes-returns"></a>

### getAttributes Returns

map: The map of attributes.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getcolumnlabels"></a>

## getColumnLabels

The `getColumnLabels` function returns the columns labels of a matrix.
The column labels can be optionally set by any function that returns a matrix.
The column labels can also be set via the `setColumnLabels` function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getcolumnlabels-parameters"></a>

### getColumnLabels Parameters

- `matrix`: The matrix to return the column labels of.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getcolumnlabels-syntax"></a>

### getColumnLabels Syntax

getColumnLabels(matrix)

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getcolumnlabels-returns"></a>

### getColumnLabels Returns

string array: The labels for each column in the matrix

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getrowlabels"></a>

## getRowLabels

The `getRowLabels` function returns the row labels of a matrix.
The row labels can be optionally set by any function that returns a matrix.
The row labels can also be set via the `setRowLabels` function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getrowlabels-parameters"></a>

### getRowLabels Parameters

- `matrix`: The matrix to return the row labels from.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getrowlabels-syntax"></a>

### getRowLabels Syntax

getRowLabels(matrix)

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getrowlabels-returns"></a>

### getRowLabels Returns

string array: The labels for each row in the matrix

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getvalue"></a>

## getValue

The `getValue` function returns the value of a single tuple entry by key.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getvalue-parameters"></a>

### getValue Parameters

- `tuple`: The tuple to return the entry from.
- `key`: The key of the entry to return the value for.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getvalue-syntax"></a>

### getValue Syntax

getValue(tuple, key)

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--getvalue-returns"></a>

### getValue Returns

object: Returns an object of the same type as the tuple entry.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--grandsum"></a>

## grandSum

The `grandSum` function sums all the values in a matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--grandsum-parameters"></a>

### grandSum Parameters

- `matrix`: The matrix to operate on.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--grandsum-syntax"></a>

### grandSum Syntax

```text
grandSum(matrix)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--grandsum-returns"></a>

### grandSum Returns

number: the sum of all the values in the matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gt"></a>

## gt

The `gt` function will return whether the first parameter is greater than the second parameter.
The function accepts numeric or string parameters, but will fail to execute if all the parameters are not of the same type.
That is, all are String or all are Numeric.
If any parameters are null then an error will be raised.
Returns a boolean value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gt-parameters"></a>

### gt Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gt-syntax"></a>

### gt Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `gt` evaluator.

```text
gt(1,2) // 1 > 2
gt(1,fieldA) // 1 > fieldA
gt(fieldA,val(foo)) // fieldA > "foo"
gt(add(fieldA,fieldB),6) // fieldA + fieldB > 6
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gteq"></a>

## gteq

The `gteq` function will return whether the first parameter is greater than or equal to the second parameter.
The function accepts numeric and string parameters, but will fail to execute if all the parameters are not of the same type.
That is, all are String or all are Numeric.
If any parameters are null then an error will be raised.
Returns a boolean value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gteq-parameters"></a>

### gteq Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gteq-syntax"></a>

### gteq Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `gteq` evaluator.

```text
gteq(1,2) // 1 >= 2
gteq(1,fieldA) // 1 >= fieldA
gteq(fieldA,val(foo)) fieldA >= "foo"
gteq(add(fieldA,fieldB),6) // fieldA + fieldB >= 6
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--hist"></a>

## hist

The `hist` function creates a histogram from a numeric array.
The hist function is designed to work with continuous variables.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--hist-parameters"></a>

### hist Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `bins`: The number of bins in the histogram.
  Each returned tuple contains summary statistics for the observations that were within the bin.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--hist-syntax"></a>

### hist Syntax

```text
hist(numericArray, bins)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--hsin"></a>

## hsin

The `hsin` function returns the trigonometric hyperbolic sine of a number.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--hsin-parameters"></a>

### hsin Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`: The value to return the hyperbolic sine of.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--hsin-syntax"></a>

### hsin Syntax

```text
hsin(100.4)  // returns the hsine of 100.4
hsin(fieldA) // returns the hsine for fieldA.
if(gt(fieldA,fieldB),sin(fieldA),sin(fieldB)) // if fieldA > fieldB then return the hsine of fieldA, else return the hsine of fieldB
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--if"></a>

## if

The `if` function works like a standard conditional if/then statement.
If the first parameter is true, then the second parameter will be returned, else the third parameter will be returned.
The function accepts a boolean as the first parameter and anything as the second and third parameters.
An error will occur if the first parameter is not a boolean or is null.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--if-parameters"></a>

### if Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Value | Boolean Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--if-syntax"></a>

### if Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `if` evaluator.

```text
if(fieldA,fieldB,fieldC) // if fieldA is true then fieldB else fieldC
if(gt(fieldA,5), fieldA, 5) // if fieldA > 5 then fieldA else 5
if(eq(fieldB,null), null, div(fieldA,fieldB)) // if fieldB is null then null else fieldA / fieldB
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--indexof"></a>

## indexOf

The `indexOf` function returns the index of a string in an array of strings.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--indexof-parameters"></a>

### indexOf Parameters

- `string array`: The array to operate on.
- `string`: The string to search for in the array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--indexof-syntax"></a>

### indexOf Syntax

```text
indexOf(stringArray, string)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--indexof-returns"></a>

### indexOf Returns

integer: The index of the string in the array or -1 if the string is not found.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--integrate"></a>

## integrate

The `integrate` function computes the integral of an interpolation function for a specific range of the curve.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--integrate-parameters"></a>

### integrate Parameters

- `spline` | `akima` | `lerp` | `loess`: The interpolation function to compute the integral for.
- `numeric`: start of integral range
- `numeric`: end of integral range

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--integrate-syntax"></a>

### integrate Syntax

```text
integrate(function, start, end)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--integrate-returns"></a>

### integrate Returns

numeric: The integral

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--length"></a>

## length

The `length` function returns the length of a numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--length-parameters"></a>

### length Parameters

- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--length-syntax"></a>

### length Syntax

```text
length(numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lerp"></a>

## lerp

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--loess"></a>

## loess

The `leoss` function is a smoothing curve fitter which uses a [local regression](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Local_regression) algorithm.
Unlike the [spline](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--spline) function which touches each control point, the `loess` function puts a smooth curve through the control points without having to touch the control points.
The `loess` result can be used by the [derivative](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--derivative) function to produce smooth derivatives from data that is not smooth.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--loess-positional-parameters"></a>

### loess Positional Parameters

- `numeric array`: (Optional) x values.
  If omitted a sequence will be created for the x values.
- `numeric array`: y values

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--loess-named-parameters"></a>

### loess Named Parameters

- `bandwidth`: (Optional) The percent of the data points to use when drawing the local regression line, defaults to .25.
  Decreasing the bandwidth increases the number of curves that loess can fit.
- `robustIterations`: (Optional) The number of iterations used to smooth outliers, defaults to 2.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--loess-syntax"></a>

### loess Syntax

```text
loess(yValues) // This creates the xValues automatically and fits a smooth curve through the data points.
loess(xValues, yValues) // This will fit a smooth curve through the data points.
loess(xValues, yValues, bandwidth=.15) // This will fit a smooth curve through the data points using 15 percent of the data points for each local regression line.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--loess-returns"></a>

### loess Returns

function: The function can be treated as both a `numeric array` of the smoothed data points and `function`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--log"></a>

## log

The `log` function will return the natural log of the provided single parameter.
The `log` function will fail to execute if the value is non-numeric.
If a null value is found, then null will be returned as the result.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--log-parameters"></a>

### log Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--log-syntax"></a>

### log Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `log` evaluator.
Only one parameter is accepted.
Returns a numeric value.

```text
log(100)
log(add(fieldA,fieldB))
log(fieldA)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lognormaldistribution"></a>

## logNormalDistribution

The `logNormalDistribution` function returns a [log normal probability distribution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Log-normal_distribution) based on its parameters.
This function is part of the probability distribution framework and is designed to work with the `sample`, `kolmogorovSmirnov` and `cumulativeProbability` functions.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lognormaldistribution-parameters"></a>

### logNormalDistribution Parameters

- `double`: shape
- `double`: scale

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lognormaldistribution-returns"></a>

### logNormalDistribution Returns

A probability distribution function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lognormaldistribution-syntax"></a>

### logNormalDistribution Syntax

```text
logNormalDistribution(.3, .0)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--kolmogorovsmirnov"></a>

## kolmogorovSmirnov

The `kolmogorovSmirnov` function performs a [Kolmogorov Smirnov test](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kolmogorov%E2%80%93Smirnov_test), between a reference continuous probability distribution and a sample set.

The supported distribution functions are: `empiricalDistribution`, `normalDistribution`, `logNormalDistribution`, `weibullDistribution`, `gammaDistribution`, and `betaDistribution`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--kolmogorovsmirnov-parameters"></a>

### kolmogorovSmirnov Parameters

- `continuous probability distribution`: Reference distribution
- `numeric array`: sample set

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--kolmogorovsmirnov-returns"></a>

### kolmogorovSmirnov Returns

A result tuple: A tuple containing the p-value and d-statistic for the test result.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--kolmogorovsmirnov-syntax"></a>

### kolmogorovSmirnov Syntax

```text
kolmogorovSmirnov(normalDistribution(10, 2), sampleSet)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lt"></a>

## lt

The `lt` function will return whether the first parameter is less than the second parameter.
The function accepts numeric or string parameters, but will fail to execute if all the parameters are not of the same type.
That is, all are String or all are Numeric.
If any parameters are null then an error will be raised.
Returns a boolean value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lt-parameters"></a>

### lt Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lt-syntax"></a>

### lt Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `lt` evaluator.

```text
lt(1,2) // 1 < 2
lt(1,fieldA) // 1 < fieldA
lt(fieldA,val(foo)) fieldA < "foo"
lt(add(fieldA,fieldB),6) // fieldA + fieldB < 6
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lteq"></a>

## lteq

The `lteq` function will return whether the first parameter is less than or equal to the second parameter.
The function accepts numeric and string parameters, but will fail to execute if all the parameters are not of the same type.
That is, all are String or all are Numeric.
If any parameters are null then an error will be raised.
Returns a boolean value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lteq-parameters"></a>

### lteq Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Value | Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lteq-syntax"></a>

### lteq Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `lteq` evaluator.

```text
lteq(1,2) // 1 <= 2
lteq(1,fieldA) // 1 <= fieldA
lteq(fieldA,val(foo)) fieldA <= "foo"
lteq(add(fieldA,fieldB),6) // fieldA + fieldB <= 6
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--markovchain"></a>

## markovChain

The `markovChain` function can be used to perform [Markov Chain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Markov_chain) simulations.
The `markovChain` function takes as its parameter a [transition matrix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stochastic_matrix) and returns a mathematical model that can be sampled using the [sample](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sample) function.
Each sample taken from the Markov Chain represents the current state of system.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--markovchain-parameters"></a>

### markovChain Parameters

- `matrix`: Transition matrix

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--markovchain-syntax"></a>

### markovChain Syntax

```text
sample(markovChain(transitionMatrix), 5)  // This creates a Markov Chain given a specific transition matrix.
The sample function takes 5 samples from the Markov Chain, representing the next five states of the system.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--markovchain-returns"></a>

### markovChain Returns

Markov Chain model: The Markoff Chain model can be used with [sample](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sample) function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--matrix"></a>

## matrix

The matrix function returns a [matrix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Matrix_(mathematics)) which can be operated on by functions that support matrix operations.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--matrix-parameters"></a>

### matrix Parameters

- `numeric array` …: One or more numeric arrays that will be the rows of the matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--matrix-syntax"></a>

### matrix Syntax

```text
matrix(numericArray1, numericArray2, numericArray3) // Returns a matrix with three rows of data: numericaArray1, numericArray2, numericArray3
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--matrix-returns"></a>

### matrix Returns

matrix

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--meandifference"></a>

## meanDifference

The `meanDifference` function calculates the mean of the differences following the element-by-element subtraction between two numeric arrays.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--meandifference-parameters"></a>

### meanDifference Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--meandifference-returns"></a>

### meanDifference Returns

A numeric.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--meandifference-syntax"></a>

### meanDifference Syntax

```text
meanDifference(numericArray, numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--minmaxscale"></a>

## minMaxScale

The `minMaxScale` function scales numeric arrays within a minimum and maximum value.
By default `minMaxScale` scales between 0 and 1.
The `minMaxScale` function can operate on both numeric arrays and matrices.

When operating on a matrix the `minMaxScale` function operates on each row of the matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--minmaxscale-parameters"></a>

### minMaxScale Parameters

- `numeric array` | `matrix`: The array or matrix to scale
- `double`: (Optional) The min value.
  Defaults to 0.
- `double`: (Optional) The max value.
  Defaults to 1.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--minmaxscale-syntax"></a>

### minMaxScale Syntax

```text
minMaxScale(numericArray) // scale a numeric array between 0 and 1
minMaxScale(numericArray, 0, 100) // scale a numeric array between 1 and 100
minMaxScale(matrix) // Scale each row in a matrix between 0 and 1
minMaxScale(matrix, 0, 100) // Scale each row in a matrix between 0 and 100
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--minmaxscale-returns"></a>

### minMaxScale Returns

A numeric array or matrix

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--mod"></a>

## mod

The `mod` function returns the remainder (modulo) of the first parameter divided by the second parameter.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--mod-parameters"></a>

### mod Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`: Parameter 1
- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`: Parameter 2

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--mod-syntax"></a>

### mod Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `mod` evaluator.

```text
mod(100,3) // returns the remainder of 100 / 3 .
mod(100,fieldA) // returns the remainder of 100 divided by the value of fieldA.
mod(fieldA,1.4) // returns the remainder of fieldA divided by 1.4.
if(gt(fieldA,fieldB),mod(fieldA,fieldB),mod(fieldB,fieldA)) // if fieldA > fieldB then return the remainder of fieldA/fieldB, else return the remainder of fieldB/fieldA.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--montecarlo"></a>

## monteCarlo

The `monteCarlo` function performs a [Monte Carlo simulation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monte_Carlo_method) based on its parameters.
The `monteCarlo` function runs another function a specified number of times and returns the results.
The function being run typically has one or more variables that are drawn from probability distributions on each run.
The `sample` function is used in the function to draw the samples.

The simulation’s result array can then be treated as an empirical distribution to understand the probabilities of the simulation results.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--montecarlo-parameters"></a>

### monteCarlo Parameters

- `numeric function`: The function being run by the simulation, which must return a numeric value.
- `integer`: The number of times to run the function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--montecarlo-returns"></a>

### monteCarlo Returns

A numeric array: The results of simulation runs.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--montecarlo-syntax"></a>

### monteCarlo Syntax

```text
let(a=uniformIntegerDistribution(1, 6),
    b=uniformIntegerDistribution(1, 6),
    c=monteCarlo(add(sample(a), sample(b)), 1000))
```

In the expression above the `monteCarlo` function is running the function `add(sample(a), sample(b))`
1000 times and returning the result.
Each time the function is run samples are drawn from the probability distributions stored in variables `a` and `b`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--movingavg"></a>

## movingAvg

The `movingAvg` function calculates a [moving average](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moving_average) over an array of numbers.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--movingavg-parameters"></a>

### movingAvg Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `window size`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--movingavg-returns"></a>

### movingAvg Returns

A numeric array.
The first element of the returned array will start from the windowSize-1 index of the original array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--movingavg-syntax"></a>

### movingAvg Syntax

```text
movingAverage(numericArray, 30)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--movingmedian"></a>

## movingMedian

The `movingMedian` function calculates a moving median over an array of numbers.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--movingmedian-parameters"></a>

### movingMedian Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `window size`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--movingmedian-returns"></a>

### movingMedian Returns

A numeric array.
The first element of the returned array will start from the windowSize-1 index of the original array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--movingmedian-syntax"></a>

### movingMedian Syntax

```text
movingMedian(numericArray, 30)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--mult"></a>

## mult

The `mult` function will take two or more numeric values and multiply them together.
The `mult` function will fail to execute if any of the values are non-numeric.
If a null value is found then null will be returned as the result.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--mult-parameters"></a>

### mult Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`
- `……`
- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--mult-syntax"></a>

### mult Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `mult` evaluator.
The number and order of these parameters do not matter and is not limited except that at least two parameters are required.
Returns a numeric value.

```text
mult(1,2,3,4) // 1 * 2 * 3 * 4
mult(1,fieldA) // 1 * value of fieldA
mult(fieldA,1.4) // value of fieldA * 1.4
mult(fieldA,fieldB,fieldC) // value of fieldA * value of fieldB * value of fieldC
mult(fieldA,div(fieldA,fieldB)) // value of fieldA * (value of fieldA / value of fieldB)
mult(fieldA,if(gt(fieldA,fieldB),fieldA,fieldB)) // if fieldA > fieldB then fieldA * fieldA, else fieldA * fieldB
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--normaldistribution"></a>

## normalDistribution

The `normalDistribution` function returns a [normal probability distribution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Normal_distribution)
based on its parameters.
This function is part of the probability distribution framework and is designed to work with the `sample`, `kolmogorovSmirnov` and `cumulativeProbability` functions.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--normaldistribution-parameters"></a>

### normalDistribution Parameters

- `double`: mean
- `double`: standard deviation

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--normaldistribution-returns"></a>

### normalDistribution Returns

A probability distribution function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--normaldistribution-syntax"></a>

### normalDistribution Syntax

```text
normalDistribution(mean, stddev)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--normalizesum"></a>

## normalizeSum

The `normalizeSum` function scales numeric arrays so that they sum to 1.
The `normalizeSum` function can operate on both numeric arrays and matrices.

When operating on a matrix the `normalizeSum` function operates on each row of the matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--normalizesum-parameters"></a>

### normalizeSum Parameters

- `numeric array` | `matrix`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--normalizesum-syntax"></a>

### normalizeSum Syntax

```text
normalizeSum(numericArray)
normalizeSum(matrix)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--normalizesum-returns"></a>

### normalizeSum Returns

numeric array | matrix

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--not"></a>

## not

The `not` function will return the logical NOT of a single boolean parameter.
The function will fail to execute if the parameter is non-boolean or null.
Returns a boolean value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--not-parameters"></a>

### not Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Boolean | Boolean Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--not-syntax"></a>

### not Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `not` evaluator.
Only one parameter is allowed.

```text
not(true) // false
not(fieldA) // true if fieldA is false else false
not(eq(fieldA,fieldB)) // true if fieldA != fieldB
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--olsregress"></a>

## olsRegress

The `olsRegress` function performs [ordinary least squares](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ordinary_least_squares), multivariate, linear regression.

The `olsRegress` function returns a single tuple containing the regression model with estimated regression parameters, RSquared and regression diagnostics.

The output of `olsRegress` can be used with the [predict](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--predict) function to predict values based on the regression model.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--olsregress-parameters"></a>

### olsRegress Parameters

- `matrix`: The regressor observation matrix.
  Each row in the matrix represents a single multi-variate regressor observation.
  Note that there is no need to add an initial unitary column (column of 1’s) when specifying a model including an intercept term, this column will be added automatically.
- `numeric array`: The outcomes array which matches up with each row in the regressor observation matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--olsregress-syntax"></a>

### olsRegress Syntax

```text
olsRegress(matrix, numericArray) // This performs the olsRegression analysis on given regressor matrix and outcome array.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--olsregress-returns"></a>

### olsRegress Returns

Tuple: The regression model including the estimated regression parameters and diagnostics.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--or"></a>

## or

The `or` function will return the logical OR of at least 2 boolean parameters.
The function will fail to execute if any parameters are non-boolean or null.
Returns a boolean value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--or-parameters"></a>

### or Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Boolean | Boolean Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Boolean | Boolean Evaluator`
- `……`
- `Field Name | Raw Boolean | Boolean Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--or-syntax"></a>

### or Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `or` evaluator.
At least two parameters are required, but there is no limit to how many you can use.

```text
or(true,fieldA) // true || fieldA
or(fieldA,fieldB) // fieldA || fieldB
or(and(fieldA,fieldB),fieldC) // (fieldA && fieldB) || fieldC
or(fieldA,fieldB,fieldC,and(fieldD,fieldE),fieldF)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--poissondistribution"></a>

## poissonDistribution

The `poissonDistribution` function returns a [poisson probability distribution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poisson_distribution)
based on its parameter.
This function is part of the probability distribution framework and is designed to work with the `sample`, `probability` and `cumulativeProbability` functions.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--poissondistribution-parameters"></a>

### poissonDistribution Parameters

- `double`: mean

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--poissondistribution-returns"></a>

### poissonDistribution Returns

A probability distribution function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--poissondistribution-syntax"></a>

### poissonDistribution Syntax

```text
poissonDistribution(mean)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--polyfit"></a>

## polyFit

The `polyFit` function performs [polynomial curve fitting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curve_fitting#Fitting_lines_and_polynomial_functions_to_data_points).

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--polyfit-parameters"></a>

### polyFit Parameters

- `numeric array`: (Optional) x values.
  If omitted a sequence will be created for the x values.
- `numeric array`: y values
- `integer`: (Optional) polynomial degree.
  Defaults to 3.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--polyfit-returns"></a>

### polyFit Returns

A numeric array: curve that was fit to the data points.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--polyfit-syntax"></a>

### polyFit Syntax

```text
polyFit(yValues) // This creates the xValues automatically and fits a curve through the data points using the default 3 degree polynomial.
polyFit(yValues, 5) // This creates the xValues automatically and fits a curve through the data points using a 5 degree polynomial.
polyFit(xValues, yValues, 5) // This will fit a curve through the data points using a 5 degree polynomial.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--pow"></a>

## pow

The `pow` function returns the value of its first parameter raised to the power of its second parameter.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--pow-parameters"></a>

### pow Parameters

- `Field Name` | `Raw Number` | `Number Evaluator`: Parameter 1
- `Field Name` | `Raw Number` | `Number Evaluator`: Parameter 2

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--pow-syntax"></a>

### pow Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `pow` evaluator.

```text
pow(2,3) // returns 2 raised to the 3rd power.
pow(4,fieldA) // returns 4 raised by the value of fieldA.
pow(fieldA,1.4) // returns the value of fieldA raised by 1.4.
if(gt(fieldA,fieldB),pow(fieldA,fieldB),pow(fieldB,fieldA)) // if fieldA > fieldB then raise fieldA by fieldB, else raise fieldB by fieldA.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--predict"></a>

## predict

The `predict` function predicts the value of dependent variables based on regression models or functions.

The `predict` function can predict values based on the output of the following functions: [spline](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--spline), [loess](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--loess), [regress](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--regress), [olsRegress](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--olsregress).

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--predict-parameters"></a>

### predict Parameters

- `regression model` | `function`: The model or function used for the prediction
- `number` | `numeric array` | `matrix`: Depending on the regression model or function used, the predictor variable can be a number, numeric array or matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--predict-syntax"></a>

### predict Syntax

```text
predict(regressModel, number) // predict using the output of the <<regress>> function and single numeric predictor. This will return a single numeric prediction.

predict(regressModel, numericArray) // predict using the output of the <<regress>> function and a numeric array of predictors. This will return a numeric array of predictions.

predict(splineFunc, number) // predict using the output of the <<spline>> function and single numeric predictor. This will return a single numeric prediction.

predict(splineFunc, numericArray) // predict using the output of the <<spline>> function and a numeric array of predictors. This will return a numeric array of predictions.

predict(olsRegressModel, numericArray) // predict using the output of the <<olsRegress>> function and a numeric array containing one multi-variate predictor. This will return a single numeric prediction.

predict(olsRegressModel, matrix) // predict using the output of the <<olsRegress>> function and a matrix containing rows of multi-variate predictor arrays. This will return a numeric array of predictions.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--primes"></a>

## primes

The `primes` function returns an array of prime numbers starting from a specified number.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--primes-parameters"></a>

### primes Parameters

- `integer`: The number of primes to return in the list
- `integer`: The starting point for returning the primes

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--primes-returns"></a>

### primes Returns

A numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--primes-syntax"></a>

### primes Syntax

```text
primes(100, 2000) // returns 100 primes starting from 2000
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--probability"></a>

## probability

The `probability` function returns the probability of a random variable within a probability distribution.

The `probability` function computes the probability between random variable ranges for both [continuous](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Probability_distribution#Continuous_probability_distribution) and
[discrete](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Probability_distribution#Discrete_probability_distribution) probability distributions.

The `probability` function can compute probabilities for a specific random variable for
discrete probability distributions only.

The supported continuous distribution functions are:
[normalDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--normaldistribution), [logNormalDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--lognormaldistribution), [betaDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--betadistribution), [gammaDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--gammadistribution), [empiricalDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--empiricaldistribution), [triangularDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--triangulardistribution), [weibullDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--weibulldistribution), [uniformDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--uniformdistribution), [constantDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--constantdistribution)

The supported discrete distributions are:
[poissonDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--poissondistribution), [binomialDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--binomialdistribution), [enumeratedDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--enumerateddistribution), [zipFDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--zipfdistribution), [geometricDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--geometricdistribution), [uniformIntegerDistribution](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--uniformintegerdistribution)

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--probability-parameters"></a>

### probability Parameters

- `probability distribution`: the probability distribution to compute the probability from.
- `number`: low value of the range.
- `number`: (Optional for discrete probability distributions) high value of the range.
  If the high range is omitted then the probability function will compute a probability for the low range value.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--probability-syntax"></a>

### probability Syntax

```text
probability(poissonDistribution(10), 7) // Returns the probability of a random sample of 7 in a poisson distribution with a mean of 10.

probability(normalDistribution(10, 2), 7.5, 8.5) // Returns the probability between the range of 7.5 to 8.5 for a normal distribution with a mean of 10 and standard deviation of 2.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--probability-returns"></a>

### probability Returns

double: probability

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rank"></a>

## rank

The `rank` performs a rank transformation on a numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rank-parameters"></a>

### rank Parameters

- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rank-syntax"></a>

### rank Syntax

```text
rank(numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--raw"></a>

## raw

The `raw` function will return whatever raw value is the parameter.
This is useful for cases where you want to use a string as part of another evaluator.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--raw-parameters"></a>

### raw Parameters

- `Raw Value`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--raw-syntax"></a>

### raw Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `raw` evaluator.
Whatever is inside will be returned as-is.
Internal evaluators are considered strings and are not evaluated.

```text
raw(foo) // "foo"
raw(count(*)) // "count(*)"
raw(45) // 45
raw(true) // "true" (note: this returns the string "true" and not the boolean true)
eq(raw(fieldA), fieldA) // true if the value of fieldA equals the string "fieldA"
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--regress"></a>

## regress

The `regress` function performs a simple regression of two numeric arrays.

The result of this expression is also used by the `predict` function.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--regress-parameters"></a>

### regress Parameters

- `numeric array`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--regress-syntax"></a>

### regress Syntax

```text
regress(numericArray1, numericArray2)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rev"></a>

## rev

The `rev` function reverses the order of a numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rev-parameters"></a>

### rev Parameters

- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rev-syntax"></a>

### rev Syntax

```text
rev(numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--round"></a>

## round

The `round` function returns the closest whole number to the argument.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--round-parameters"></a>

### round Parameters

- `Field Name` | `Raw Number` | `Number Evaluator`: The value to return the square root of.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--round-syntax"></a>

### round Syntax

```text
round(100.4)
round(fieldA)
if(gt(fieldA,fieldB),sqrt(fieldA),sqrt(fieldB)) // if fieldA > fieldB then return the round of fieldA, else return the round of fieldB
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rowat"></a>

## rowAt

The `rowAt` function returns the row of a matrix at a specific index as a numeric array.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rowat-parameters"></a>

### rowAt Parameters

- `matrix`: the matrix to operate on
- `integer`: the index of the row to return

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rowat-syntax"></a>

### rowAt Syntax

```text
rowAt(matrix, 10)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rowat-returns"></a>

### rowAt Returns

numeric array: the row of the matrix

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rowcount"></a>

## rowCount

The `rowCount` function returns the number of rows in a `matrix`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rowcount-parameters"></a>

### rowCount Parameters

- `matrix`: the matrix to operate on

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rowcount-syntax"></a>

### rowCount Syntax

```text
rowCount(matrix)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--rowcount-returns"></a>

### rowCount Returns

integer: number rows in the matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sample"></a>

## sample

The `sample` function can be used to draw random samples from a probability distribution or Markov Chain.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sample-parameters"></a>

### sample Parameters

- `probability distribution` | `Markov Chain`: The distribution or Markov Chain to sample.
- `integer`: (Optional) Sample size.
  Defaults to 1.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sample-returns"></a>

### sample Returns

Either a single numeric random sample, or a numeric array depending on the sample size parameter.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sample-syntax"></a>

### sample Syntax

```text
sample(poissonDistribution(5)) // Returns a single random sample from a poissonDistribution with mean of 5.
sample(poissonDistribution(5), 1000) // Returns 1000 random samples from poissonDistribution with a mean of 5.
sample(markovChain(transitionMatrix), 1000) // Returns 1000 random samples from a Markov Chain.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalaradd"></a>

## scalarAdd

The `scalarAdd` function adds a scalar value to every value in a numeric array or matrix.
When working with numeric arrays, `scalarAdd` returns a new array with the new values.
When working with a matrix, `scalarAdd` returns a new matrix with new values.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalaradd-parameters"></a>

### scalarAdd Parameters

`number`: value to add
`numeric array` | `matrix`: the numeric array or matrix to add the value to.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalaradd-syntax"></a>

### scalarAdd Syntax

```text
scalarAdd(number, numericArray) // Adds the number to each element in the number in the array.
scalarAdd(number, matrix) // Adds the number to each value in a matrix
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalaradd-returns"></a>

### scalarAdd Returns

numericArray | matrix: Depending on what is being operated on.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalardivide"></a>

## scalarDivide

The `scalarDivide` function divides each number in numeric array or matrix by a scalar value.
When working with numeric arrays, `scalarDivide` returns a new array with the new values.
When working with a matrix, `scalarDivide` returns a new matrix with new values.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalardivide-parameters"></a>

### scalarDivide Parameters

`number`: value to divide by
`numeric array` | `matrix`: the numeric array or matrix to divide by the value to.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalardivide-syntax"></a>

### scalarDivide Syntax

```text
scalarDivide(number, numericArray) // Divides each element in the numeric array by the number.
scalarDivide(number, matrix) // Divides each element in the matrix by the number.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalardivide-returns"></a>

### scalarDivide Returns

numericArray | matrix: depending on what is being operated on.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalarmultiply"></a>

## scalarMultiply

The `scalarMultiply` function multiplies each element in a numeric array or matrix by a
scalar value.
When working with numeric arrays, `scalarMultiply` returns a new array with the new values.
When working with a matrix, `scalarMultiply` returns a new matrix with new values.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalarmultiply-parameters"></a>

### scalarMultiply Parameters

`number`: value to divide by
`numeric array` | `matrix`: the numeric array or matrix to divide by the value to.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalarmultiply-syntax"></a>

### scalarMultiply Syntax

```text
scalarMultiply(number, numericArray) // Multiplies each element in the numeric array by the number.
scalarMultiply(number, matrix) // Multiplies each element in the matrix by the number.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalarmultiply-returns"></a>

### scalarMultiply Returns

numericArray | matrix: depending on what is being operated on

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalarsubtract"></a>

## scalarSubtract

The `scalarSubtract` function subtracts a scalar value from every value in a numeric array or matrix.
When working with numeric arrays, `scalarSubtract` returns a new array with the new values.
When working with a matrix, `scalarSubtract` returns a new matrix with new values.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalarsubtract-parameters"></a>

### scalarSubtract Parameters

`number`: value to add
`numeric array` | `matrix`: the numeric array or matrix to subtract the value from.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalarsubtract-syntax"></a>

### scalarSubtract Syntax

```text
scalarSubtract(number, numericArray) // Subtracts the number from each element in the number in the array.
scalarSubtract(number, matrix) // Subtracts the number from each value in a matrix
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scalarsubtract-returns"></a>

### scalarSubtract Returns

numericArray | matrix: depending on what is being operated on.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scale"></a>

## scale

The `scale` function multiplies all the elements of an array by a number.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scale-parameters"></a>

### scale Parameters

- `number`
- `numeric array`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--scale-syntax"></a>

### scale Syntax

```text
scale(number, numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sequence"></a>

## sequence

The `sequence` function returns an array of numbers based on its parameters.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sequence-parameters"></a>

### sequence Parameters

- `length`
- `start`
- `stride`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sequence-syntax"></a>

### sequence Syntax

```text
sequence(100, 0, 1) // Returns a sequence of length 100, starting from 0 with a stride of 1.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setattributes"></a>

## setAttributes

The `setAttributes` function sets an attributes map of a `matrix`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setattributes-parameters"></a>

### setAttributes Parameters

- `matrix`: The matrix to set the attributes map to.
- `map`: The map of attributes to set on the matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setattributes-syntax"></a>

### setAttributes Syntax

```text
setAttributes(matrix, map)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setattributes-returns"></a>

### setAttributes Returns

matrix: The matrix with the attributes set.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setcolumnlabels"></a>

## setColumnLabels

The `setColumnLabels` function sets the columns labels of a matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setcolumnlabels-parameters"></a>

### setColumnLabels Parameters

- `matrix`: The matrix to set the column labels to.
- `string array`: The column labels to set the matrix

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setcolumnlabels-syntax"></a>

### setColumnLabels Syntax

setColumnLabels(matrix, labels)

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setcolumnlabels-returns"></a>

### setColumnLabels Returns

matrix: The matrix with the labels set.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setrowlabels"></a>

## setRowLabels

The `setRowLabels` function sets the row labels of a matrix.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setrowlabels-parameters"></a>

### setRowLabels Parameters

- `matrix`: The matrix to set the row labels to.
- `string array`: The row labels to set to the matrix

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setrowlabels-syntax"></a>

### setRowLabels Syntax

setRowLabels(matrix, labels)

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setrowlabels-returns"></a>

### setRowLabels Returns

matrix: The matrix with the labels set.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setvalue"></a>

## setValue

The `setValue` function sets a new value for a tuple entry.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setvalue-parameters"></a>

### setValue Parameters

- `tuple`: The tuple to return the entry from.
- `key`: The key of the entry to set.
- `value`: The value to set.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setvalue-syntax"></a>

### setValue Syntax

setValue(tuple, key, value)

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--setvalue-returns"></a>

### setValue Returns

tuple: Returns the new modified tuple

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sin"></a>

## sin

The `sin` function returns the trigonometric sine of a number.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sin-parameters"></a>

### sin Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`: The value to return the sine of.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sin-syntax"></a>

### sin Syntax

```text
sin(100.4)  // returns the sine of 100.4
sine(fieldA) // returns the sine for fieldA.
if(gt(fieldA,fieldB),sin(fieldA),sin(fieldB)) // if fieldA > fieldB then return the sine of fieldA, else return the sine of fieldB
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--spline"></a>

## spline

The `spline` function performs a cubic spline interpolation (<https://en.wikiversity.org/wiki/Cubic_Spline_Interpolation>) of a curve given a set of x,y coordinates.
The return value of the spline function is an interpolation function which can be used to [predict](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--predict) values along the curve and generate a [derivative](#query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--derivative) of
the curve.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--spline-parameters"></a>

### spline Parameters

- `numeric array`: (Optional) x values.
  If omitted a sequence will be created for the x values.
- `numeric array`: y values

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--spline-syntax"></a>

### spline Syntax

```text
spline(yValues) // This creates the xValues automatically and fits a spline through the data points.
spline(xValues, yValues) // This will fit a spline through the data points.
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--spline-returns"></a>

### spline Returns

function: the function can be treated as both a `numeric array` and `function`.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sqrt"></a>

## sqrt

The `sqrt` function returns the trigonometric square root of a number.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sqrt-parameters"></a>

### sqrt Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`: The value to return the square root of.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sqrt-syntax"></a>

### sqrt Syntax

```text
sqrt(100.4)  // returns the square root of 100.4
sqrt(fieldA) // returns the square root for fieldA.
if(gt(fieldA,fieldB),sqrt(fieldA),sqrt(fieldB)) // if fieldA > fieldB then return the sqrt of fieldA, else return the sqrt of fieldB
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--standardize"></a>

## standardize

The `standardize` function standardizes a numeric array so that values within the array have a mean of 0 and standard deviation of 1.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--standardize-parameters"></a>

### standardize Parameters

- `numeric array`: the array to standardize

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--standardize-syntax"></a>

### standardize Syntax

```text
standardize(numericArray)
```

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--standardize-returns"></a>

### standardize Returns

numeric array: the standardized values

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sub"></a>

## sub

The `sub` function will take 2 or more numeric values and subtract them, from left to right.
The `sub` function will fail to execute if any of the values are non-numeric.
If a null value is found then `null` will be returned as the result.

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sub-parameters"></a>

### sub Parameters

- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`
- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`
- `……`
- `Field Name | Raw Number | Number Evaluator`

<a id="query-guide-stream-evaluator-reference--sub-syntax"></a>

### sub Syntax

The expressions below show the various ways in which you can use the `sub` evaluator.
The number of these parameters does not matter and is not limited except that at least two parameters are required.
Returns a numeric value.

